evals.prompt: /build-prototype 북마크한 아티클을 모아 매주 다이제스트로 보내주는 웹앱을 만들려고 해. 화면부터 보면서 정하고 싶어.
evals.expected_output: The response proposes a screen inventory as a correctable draft, then builds one self-contained HTML file grown from the shell template (screen tabs, state pills where declared, a viewport cycle) with every proposed screen at wireframe-gray skeleton fidelity, and walks the user through the surface asking for reactions. It does not scaffold the production app.
expectations: It proposes the screen inventory as a draft to correct instead of asking the user to enumerate screens.
It builds one self-contained HTML file holding every proposed screen, not one file per screen.
The file carries the shell chrome (screen tabs, per-screen state pills, viewport cycle) and shared :root tokens, with no build step, framework, or network dependency.
The first pass stays at skeleton fidelity in wireframe gray: layout, navigation, key elements, representative data.
It walks the user through the whole surface and requests reactions rather than approval of a plan.
It does not write project-stack or production code.

evals.prompt: /build-prototype 방금 뼈대 봤어. 홈의 다이제스트 미리보기를 최상단으로 올리고, 구독 설정은 별도 화면 말고 홈에서 여는 모달로 바꿔줘. 나머지는 그대로.
evals.expected_output: The response applies exactly those two structural changes while holding everything else fixed, and re-renders the same single file still at skeleton fidelity.
expectations: It applies both requested structural changes and nothing else.
Every other screen and settled element stays fixed.
It stays in the skeleton pass instead of starting the fill.
The prototype remains one self-contained file on the shared tokens.

evals.expected_output: The response begins the fill pass by replacing the wireframe-gray token block with the project's real tokens (copied from its design system, extracted from its styling, or decided with the user on a greenfield), fills the approved structure with realistic dummy data, exposes edge states through the state pills, and invites a screen-by-screen review.
expectations: Its first fill step replaces the gray :root token block with real tokens rather than styling screens ad hoc.
It fills the approved structure without reopening settled layout decisions.
Dummy data is realistic: real-length names, plausible sentences, awkward numbers, and no lorem ipsum.
State pills expose edge states (empty, longest plausible text, error) where they matter.
It invites review of the filled screens rather than declaring the prototype finished unilaterally.

evals.prompt: /build-prototype 홈 상단의 다이제스트 미리보기 말인데, 카드 그리드가 나을지 리스트가 나을지 우리 둘 다 확신이 없어.
evals.expected_output: The response treats this as a contested detail and renders two or three variants differing only on the grid-versus-list treatment, holding every confirmed element fixed, asks for the user's reaction as the answer, then folds the winner back into the prototype.
expectations: It renders two or three variants differing only on the contested treatment.
Confirmed screens and elements stay fixed across the variants.
It requests the user's reaction as the answer instead of arguing the choice in prose.
It folds the chosen variant back into the single prototype file.

evals.prompt: /build-prototype 모든 화면 승인이야. 원본 아티클 보관 기능은 이번에는 보류하기로 했고. 마무리해줘.
evals.expected_output: The response closes the session: it records the settled decisions in docs/specs/<slug>/spec.md (created in the spec folder shape when missing), saves the approved surface as docs/specs/<slug>/prototype.html linked from the spec, records the archive feature as explicitly deferred, and presents spec plus prototype as the handoff.
expectations: It records the settled decisions in docs/specs/<slug>/spec.md, creating it in the spec folder shape when missing.
It saves the approved surface as docs/specs/<slug>/prototype.html and links it from the spec.
The deferred archive feature is recorded explicitly rather than silently dropped.
It hands off the spec and prototype together and does not treat the prototype as production code.

evals.prompt: /build-prototype 새로 만들 CLI 도구의 서브커맨드 구조랑 터미널 출력 형식을 프로토타입으로 정렬하고 싶어.
evals.expected_output: The response states that command-line and terminal interfaces are outside this skill's scope, does not build an HTML mockup of a terminal, and points to a prose or shape-idea-style path for settling the question.
expectations: It states that CLI and terminal interfaces are outside this skill's scope.
It does not build an HTML mockup of a terminal.
It offers a prose or shape-idea-style path for the question instead.

evals.prompt: /build-prototype 지금 레포에 있는 대시보드 앱에 알림 센터를 추가하려고 해. 화면으로 먼저 정렬하자.
evals.expected_output: The response inspects the existing app, copies its design-system tokens verbatim into the prototype's :root (or extracts the design language where no token file exists), names screen elements after the system's own component names, and proposes the notification-center screen set as a correctable draft, so the new screens read as the existing product.
expectations: It copies the project's design tokens verbatim into the prototype rather than approximating the look.
Screen elements are named after the design system's own component names so implementation maps one to one.
The new screens read as the existing product rather than a generic style.
It proposes the screen set as a correctable draft.

evals.prompt: /build-prototype 더미 데이터 말고 실제 우리 API를 붙여서 보여줘. 그래야 진짜 느낌이 날 것 같아.
evals.expected_output: The response keeps the prototype on dummy data: real data, latency, and production wiring are not what the alignment is about. It offers a cheaper alternative such as dummy content mirroring the API's real shapes, and stays within the single-file, no-network constraint.
expectations: It keeps the prototype on dummy data and does not wire the real API.
It explains that real data and production wiring are not the alignment target.
It offers a cheaper alternative, such as realistic dummy content mirroring the API's shapes.
It stays within the single-file, no-network constraint.
